content:
I assume that when you want to read the contents of a message you double-click it to open it in a new tab (or a new window). But, as I understand your description, actually this no longer happens like before in your email account in Thunderbird: instead of opening the message in a new tab Thunderbird opens a "Write" window (such as shown in my first picture) with at the bottom the yellow message you cite in your description.

This can happen when in Account Settings > Copies & Folders > Drafts > Keep draft messages in ◉ "Other" is checked and the Inbox folder of this account is erroneously chosen as destination folder (second image). So please first verify your account settings for keeping draft messages.

But there also can be another cause for your issue, when an Inbox folder has two "flags": one as Inbox and another for Drafts. To detect and handle this anomaly first install the add-on "FolderFlags" https://services.addons.thunderbird.net/En-uS/thunderbird/addon/folderflags/ Then R-click on Inbox > Properties > Flags und disable (uncheck) the flag for "Drafts"

content:
A Trash folder is not meant to be an "All Mail" folder. Mailbox files of this size can get corrupt. If you want to keep messages forever save them in Local Folders.

To recover a fluid Thunderbird I propose the following actions: • in TB go to Help > Troubleshooting information > Profile Folder > Open Folder...., then quit TB • in the profile folder go to Mail and open pop.1und1.de • rename the Trash file in Trash-old and delete the Trash.msf file • now when you restart TB it will automatically create a new empty Papierkorb folder in your 1und1 account • I assume that TB will again run fluently and and you can delete messages which will go to the new Papierkorb

As for the fate of all those old messages stored in the presumably corrupt Trash (now Trash-old) file: on restart TB automatically has created an new index file for the Trash-old folder and maybe you can again select and read messages in Trash-old in TB. If not and if you really no longer need those old messages, go back in the profile folder and delete the 17 GB Trash-old file.

If you really need those old messages and you had a Mac you could split the big 17 GB mailbox file in let's say 17 smaller mailbox files with 1 GB each in less than a minute via a simple command line in the Terminal. Since you are using a Windows PC you'd have to search a mbox splitting tool in the web. When such a tool is freeware it won't be able to split a text file as big as yours, those which can split very big files are fee-paying.

Alternatively you might try to split manually your big 17 GB Trash-old text file in several smaller text files, but first you'd have to look for a very powerful text editor and secondly manually cutting this big file in smaller text portions (select > copy> paste) is a very strenuous activity.
creator: Mapenzi

content: I encourage you to not rely on an email account for your organization's mailing. That should be done by a specialized company. Mailchimp offers discounts for non-profits, and Mailerlite is free for up to 500 email addresses. These or others are you best, and probably only, means to avoid being seen as spammers. Further, mailing organizations often provide other services. For example, I administered a mailing list or a town government and, after using Mailchimp for a few months, I discovered a number of reports, one of which showed that only 50% of recipients were opening the mail. Good luck to you.
creator: davidsk

content:
This article indicates you can install the ESR over the monthly. https://blog.thunderbird.net/2025/03/thunderbird-release-channel-update/ and removing the compatibility.ini file should suffice. However, I always recommend being safe. That is, before any significant change:

  1. exit thunderbird ]
  2. do a backup for safety

Steps for backup are here:::

  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media or another folder away from the thunderbird folder
creator: davidsk

content:
Following (I hope) your instructions and those in the linked article (and its sub-linked articles) I have:

  1. backed up Thunderbird's data (while Thunderbird was closed).
  2. Installed 140.8.1esr
  3. verified that C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird\thunderbird.exe is indeed 140.8.1
  4. started it via C:\Program Files\Mozilla Thunderbird\thunderbird.exe -allow-downgrade

My previous profile came up and I received (often overlapping) prompts for authentication of my IMAP, Calendar and contacts accounts. After logging into all of the provided prompts and authorizing Thunderbird to read my Google Workspace (GWS) Calendar and Contacts, I find that most of the IMAP accounts work (including two Google accounts). However my Apple (me.com) account fails to login, as does my GWS calendar, even though I gave Thunderbird the needed permission in the Google popup. Thanks for your help!
